title: Suicide Prevention Training Should Occur At What Prescribed Interval
source_filename: Suicide Prevention Training Should Occur At What Prescribed Interval.docx

qas.q: Training, intervention, response along with what else make up the 4 elements of the Navy's Suicide Prevention Program?

At least how often will suicide prevention training be conducted for all active component and reserve component service members, and for all Navy civilian employees and full time contractors who work on military installations?
MILPERSMAN 1770 along with which series contains guidance for reporting suicides and suicide related behaviors?
What is a self-inflicted death that has evidence, either implicit or explicit, of the intent to die?

Within how many days of notification of death shall commands complete the DOD suicide event report to report suicide instances and undetermined deaths for which suicide has not been excluded by the medical examiner?
How many weeks after a members suicide are commands advised to maintain copies of medical, dental, and service records in order to complete the DOD SER and respond to unforeseen questions?

What is a self-inflicted, potentially injurious behavior, with a known fatal outcome that may or may not result in injury and for which there is evidence, either implicit or explicit, of intent to die?
Within how many days of medical evaluation must a DOD SER be completed for all suicide attempts by AC and RC service members, as determined by competent medical authority?
Who aids the CO in determining that the suicide prevention program is fully implemented?
Who is responsible for establishing a suicide prevention policy?
qas.a:
Deputy Chief of Naval Operations
manpower personnel
training & education
CNO
N1

qas.q: Who develops the procedures and policy that ensures service members are properly evaluated and treated, that exhibits suicide related ideations, communications, or behaviors?
Who develops the written procedures to ensure that Chaplain or religious program specialists execute their suicide prevention programs responsibilities throughout the Navy?

Who ensures that installations emergency response personnel receive annual training, which reviews safety precautions and procedures, and deescalation techniques when responding to situations of potential suicide related behaviors and psychiatric emergencies?

The Commander, Naval Education and Training Command, shall include rates specific suicide intervention training at A Schools and C schools for Hospital Corpsmen, Religious Program Specialists, and which other rate?
What should the minimum rank of the SPC be whenever it is possible?
How often should CO's ensure that suicide prevention training is conducted for all command personnel?
What are any self-reported thoughts of engaging in suicide related behaviors defined as?

What is any interpersonal action, verbal or non-verbal, without a direct self-injurious component, passive or active, for which there is evidence, either explicit or implicit, that the person is communicating that is suicide-related behavior might occur in the near future?
What is a proposed method of carrying out a design that can potentially result in suicide related behaviors?
What is a self-inflicted potentially injurious called for which there is evidence, either explicit or implicit, that the person did not intend to kill themselves, that is, had no intent to die?
